sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Matthew, has been experiencing back pain for an extended period, with the pain radiating down his left leg, described as an electrical toothache.
The pain is exacerbated by standing, walking, coughing, and sneezing, and is relieved by lying down.
The patient rates his pain as a 6 out of 10 currently, but as high as 13 out of 10 when severe.
He has been unable to perform daily activities, including tying his shoes, and has been bedridden for a month.
The patient has a history of a fall from a roof 30 years ago, which may have resulted in a fracture.
Past medical history: Not mentioned.
Review of systems: The patient has numbness and tingling in both lower extremities, with weakness in both legs.
Coughing and sneezing worsen the pain.
Current medications: Not mentioned.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure, pulse rate, oxygen saturation, and temperature are within normal limits.
Physical exam: The patient has pain to palpation at the L4 vertebrae, and pain with flexion and extension of the back.
Strength in the left leg is 4 out of 5, and 5 out of 5 on the right.
Reflexes are brisk and motor and sensory are intact in both lower extremities.
Test Results: MRI results show a disk herniation at the level of L4-L5 vertebrae with nerve root impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has acute disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement, causing lower back pain and leg pain.
Problem list: Disk herniation at L4-L5; Nerve root impingement; Lower back pain; Leg pain.
Plan:
I prescribed an epidural steroid injection to reduce inflammation and relieve pain.
I will refer the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the area.
The patient will see me again in about a month to assess the effectiveness of the injection.
Follow-up: The patient will see me again in about a month to assess the effectiveness of the epidural steroid injection.
